name: Ascent to Picón Blanco — circular from Quintanilla del Rebollar
name_original: Subida al Picón Blanco — circular desde Quintanilla del Rebollar
description: This difficult 24.7-mile road cycling route from Quintanilla del Rebollar ascends to Picón Blanco, offering challenging gradients and reward
region_intro: Road cycling in Lic Ojo Guareña offers diverse terrain across a unique karst landscape, nestled on the southern slopes of the Cantabrian Mountains. The region is characterized by a varied topography, featuring mountains, lush forests of oaks and beeches, and scenic valleys carved by rivers like the Guareña and Trema. This blend of geological formations and natural environments provides a compelling setting for no traffic road cycling routes. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Lic Ojo Guareña The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Pastries from La Dolce Vita – Picón Blanco loop from Espinosa de los Monteros, a 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This route features a significant elevation gain, offering a challenging ride through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villarcayo Riverside Beach – Alcázar of the Constables loop from Espinosa de los Monteros, a moderate 44.8 miles (72.0 km) path. This route winds through picturesque countryside, passing by riverside areas and historical points of interest. Local road cyclists also love the Villarcayo Riverside Beach – Hermitage of San Pedro loop from Redondo, a 102.1 miles (164.3 km) trail leading through forests and valleys, often completed in about 7 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling in Lic Ojo Guareña is defined by its karst formations, dense woodlands, and river valleys, with routes offering options for different ability levels from moderate to difficult. The routes in Lic Ojo Guareña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 3,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lic Ojo Guareña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic road cycling routes are available in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: There are several dedicated no traffic road cycling routes in Lic Ojo Guareña. Our guide features 9 carefully selected routes, offering a range of distances and challenges for cyclists looking to explore the region's quiet roads.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of no traffic road cycling routes in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: The no traffic road cycling routes in Lic Ojo Guareña cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 6 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for everyone from casual riders to experienced cyclists.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along no traffic road cycling routes in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Lic Ojo Guareña is renowned for its unique karst landscapes, featuring a vast network of caves and underground galleries. Above ground, you'll cycle through diverse scenery including mountains, lush forests of oaks and beeches, and picturesque valleys. Keep an eye out for viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3482674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Cuerno&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the no traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. A prominent attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña&lt;/a&gt;, carved into a cave entrance, which offers guided tours. You can also explore the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Tirso and San Bernabé Caves&lt;/a&gt;, known for their geological and archaeological significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quisicedo Church&lt;/a&gt; is another notable religious building in the area.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about no traffic road cycling in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Lic Ojo Guareña, with an average score of 4.7 out of 5 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the region's quiet roads, diverse landscapes, and the opportunity to discover unique natural and cultural landmarks away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there any circular no traffic road cycling routes in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38199487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón Blanco loop from Las Cascajeras&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a rewarding ride through the scenic terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that include challenging climbs on no traffic roads?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20166264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastries from La Dolce Vita – Picón Blanco loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, including ascents to areas like Picón Blanco, known for its demanding gradients.

Q: Are there longer no traffic road cycling routes for extended rides?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a longer adventure, consider routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarcayo Riverside Beach – Hermitage of San Pedro loop from Redondo&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers over 160 kilometers and includes substantial elevation, perfect for a full day out on the bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for no traffic road cycling in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Lic Ojo Guareña benefits from a blend of Atlantic and Mediterranean climates, making spring and autumn ideal for road cycling. The weather is generally mild, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winter might offer colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or villages where I can stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes are designed to pass through or near charming towns and villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20162809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarcayo Riverside Beach – Alcázar of the Constables loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt; offers opportunities to explore Espinosa de los Monteros, where you can find cafes and other amenities.

Q: Where can I get more information about the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument?
A: For comprehensive information, maps, and guidance on exploring the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument, you can visit the Casa del Parque Ojo Guareña, located in Quintanilla del Rebollar. It serves as a visitor center for the area.
tips:
This is one of the best-known climbs in the province of Burgos, not only because it's always the star stage of the Vuelta a Burgos but also because it has appeared in key stages of the Vuelta a España, one of the three most important stage races in the world.
A brutal eight-and-a-half-kilometer climb at an average gradient of over 9%.
Hard port compared to its neighbors in the valley. Long slopes greater than 10%

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Ascent to Picón Blanco circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route is estimated to take around 2 hours and 25 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Ascent to Picón Blanco is rated as difficult. It features significant elevation gain, averaging around 9.2% over several kilometers, with sections reaching up to 12.5% and even 15%. It's best suited for cyclists with very good fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Quintanilla del Rebollar?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for Quintanilla del Rebollar are not provided, as a small village, you can typically find roadside parking or designated areas within the village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on busy days, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, due to its difficult rating, significant elevation gain of nearly 930 meters, and steep gradients, this route is not recommended for beginner road cyclists. It requires a high level of fitness and experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Picón Blanco ascent?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads, but some sections of the Picón Blanco ascent have been described as having a road surface that is 'not too good,' adding to the challenge. Expect steep climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are generally spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. During summer, it can be very hot, and in winter, snow and ice can make the higher sections impassable. Be aware that fog can reduce visibility at higher altitudes at any time of year.

faqs.question: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks will I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful views, especially in the initial stages. Key points of interest include the challenging Ascent to Picón Blanco from Espinosa de los Monteros, the Picón Blanco Summit and Abandoned Military Base with its antennas, and the La Sía Pass.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while there are no specific restrictions on dogs, it's generally not suitable for cycling with pets. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always keep them on a leash if you stop in populated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle the Ascent to Picón Blanco circular route. It uses public roads in the Burgos province.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the [CIMA BU09] Picón Blanco, [CIMA BU10] La Sía, Picón Castro – UTPC66 Ultra Trail, and Senda del Estraperlo-Domingo Pájaro, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel near the route?
faqs.answer: Quintanilla del Rebollar is a small village, so options might be limited. It's best to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride. Larger towns in the vicinity, such as Espinosa de los Monteros, would offer more choices for cafes, pubs, and restaurants.
